matches.snippet: on either wide {pardonnei/\ nodding, all the Finner Camps concurred
matches.reasoning: Direct anagram/wordplay: 'Finner' contains 'Finn' and echoes Fenians/Fianna; 'Camps' suggests the military band

matches.snippet: which our own little Graunya of the chilired cheeks dished up to the greatsire
matches.reasoning: Graunya (Gráinne) is the wife of Diarmuid in the Finn legend cycle, 'Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne'; directly evokes Finn's mythic world

matches.snippet: come leinster's even, true dotter of a dearmud, (her pitch was Forty Steps
matches.reasoning: Garbled/phonetic spelling of Diarmuid (Dearmud=Dear-mud); another reference to the Finn cycle's hero-lover Diarmuid

matches.snippet: Now by memory inspired, turn wheel again to the whole of the wall. Where Gyant Blyant fronts Peannlueamoore
matches.reasoning: 'Gyant Blyant' (Giant Bliant) invokes the giant-as-landscape motif, with 'Peannlueamoore' possibly evoking geographical features; Finn-as-sleeping-giant theme

matches.snippet: we have been compelled for the rejoicement of foinne loidies ind the humours
matches.reasoning: Anagram of 'Finn' embedded in 'foinne'; phonetically echoes 'Fianna' and old Irish mystique; 'loidies' corrupts Irish 'laoidhe' (poem/lay)
